name: git-workflow
tagline: Reference guide for git commit message format, branch naming conventions, and pull request templates.
verdict: Clean, focused git-workflow reference with concrete commit/branch/PR formats and no security concerns. Weak spots: no stated exclusions (could misfire on unrelated git questions) and zero guidance for common failures like rebase conflicts or rejected pushes.

highlights.summary: Description states what it covers but has no 'use when' trigger phrasing or example user phrasings.
No NOT for/out-of-scope/pointer-to-other-skill text anywhere in the skill.
No failure guidance for rebase conflicts, rejected pushes, or an unavailable commit_message.py script.
